perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise defined, capitalised terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment, shall have the same meaning ascribed thereto in the GMA.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise defined herein, capitalised terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment, shall have the same meaning ascribed thereto in the GMA. However, the term 'Services' as used in Clause 2.1(i) shall refer to services outlined in Exhibit 14, and as used in Clause 4.4 shall refer to services provided under separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Services'. Initially, all capitalized terms refer back to the GMA definitions, creating consistency. The perturbation introduces a second, distinct meaning to the term 'Services', depending on the clause. This creates an inconsistency because the reader is no longer able to use a singular definition when reading through the document. This internal contradiction in the definition of 'Services' within the document creates confusion and undermines contract enforcement by allowing multiple interpretations of the term in different sections, leading to potential legal disputes over meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Disclosing Party's proprietary rights Any Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The Amendment shall not be construed as granting or conferring to the Receiving Party, either expressly or by implication, any license or proprietary interest in or to any Confidential Information nor any right of use beyond the purpose of this Amendment. The Repairer, its Affiliates and/or its Subcontractors as applicable shall remain the exclusive owner of any intellectual property right related to the Services including: design of the LRUs, Main Elements, job cards, task cards, operating manual or industrial process, as relevant. No title to or other ownership interest in
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by defining 'Services' differently depending on which clause of the amendment is being referred to. The original text defers to the GMA for the definition of all capitalized terms, including Services. The change overrides this general rule, providing two additional distinct definitions of 'Services' in clauses 2.1(i) and 4.4 of the Amendment. Because there are now contradictory definitions of 'Services', the meaning of 'Services' in section 4.4 becomes ambiguous and unenforceable. For example, if intellectual property rights are linked to the 'Services' as defined in exhibit 14, it is inconsistent with the statement that "The Repairer, its Affiliates and/or its Subcontractors as applicable shall remain the exclusive owner of any intellectual property right related to the Services". The change leads to confusion, because there is no indication of which 'Services' the intellectual property rights apply to. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities' definition of introducing contradiction in the text.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall enter into force on the date of its signature by both Parties and, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by the Parties through a subsequent amendment to the GMA, shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall enter into force on the date of its signature by both Parties and, unless otherwise agreed upon orally by the Parties through a subsequent discussion and mutual understanding, shall remain in force for a period of two years from the date of commencement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction regarding the Amendment's duration. Originally, it remains in force for the term of the GMA, and amendments must be in writing. With the modification, the duration is set for two years with amendments possible orally. This contradicts the clause's own statement that all amendments must be in writing (6.3) and leads to interpretation issues of contract longevity and modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 This Amendment shall not be varied or modified except by a written document duly signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the amendment can be modified orally ('unless otherwise agreed upon orally by the Parties through a subsequent discussion and mutual understanding'), while the contradicted text (6.3) explicitly states that all modifications must be in writing ('This Amendment shall not be varied or modified except by a written document duly signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties'). This directly conflicts and creates uncertainty about how the amendment can be modified, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall make their reasonable endeavours to settle the Dispute amicably by a committee composed of one (1) management representative of each Party (the "Representatives").
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall make their best efforts to settle the Dispute amicably by a committee composed of one (1) legal representative of each Party (the "Representatives").
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the term from 'reasonable endeavors' to 'best efforts,' and from 'management representative' to 'legal representative.' 'Reasonable endeavors' implies a good-faith effort that does not require unreasonable sacrifices, while 'best efforts' suggests a more stringent obligation. Also, it changes the composition of the committee. This causes a contradiction with what is said on section 5.2.1 where the representatives are referred as (i) fail to create such committee or (ii) do not agree on an amicable settlement within [*****] from the date the committee referred to in this sub-Clause 5.2 has been created, it will depend now on legal representatives instead of management ones. The original text does not mention who they should be, but the pertubation in 5.2 makes it have in-text contradition with 5.2.1 .
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2.1 Subject to sub-Clause 5.2.5 below and in the event the Representatives (i) fail to create such committee or (ii) do not agree on an amicable settlement within [*****] from the date the committee referred to in this sub-Clause 5.2 has been created or such longer period as may be agreed upon in writing by the Representatives (the "Amicable Settlement Period"), the Dispute shall be exclusively and finally settled under the Rules and Conciliation of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce (the "ICC") by an arbitral tribunal composed of three (3) arbitrators; each Party shall then appoint one (1) arbitrator within [*****]from the last day of the Amicable Settlement Period and the third arbitrator, who will act as President, will be appointed by the other two (2) arbitrators. In case the two (2) arbitrators appointed by the Parties do not agree on this choice with [*****] from the date the last arbitrator is appointed, the third arbitrator will be appointed by the ICC Court.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction by changing the representatives involved in the dispute resolution process from 'management representatives' to 'legal representatives.' This directly conflicts with clause 5.2.1, which refers to the representatives as 'Representatives' without specifying their designation and is now misaligned because the representatives are now specified. This creates a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction.
